title: MCP server over SQL — a JSON-RPC call is an ncurl POST (JSON response)
description: An MCP (Model Context Protocol) server call as a MANIFEST, zero TypeScript. MCP is JSON-RPC 2.0 over HTTP, so `initialize` / `tools/list` / `tools/call` is a ducknng_ncurl_table POST. This example is structurally validated here; `test/ducknng-sql-http.test.ts` proves the local session-header loop with a ducknng MCP-style route (`initialize` returns `Mcp-Session-Id`, the next `tools/list` call sends it back). This path requests `Accept: application/json` and materializes the JSON-RPC RESPONSE into a table; it does NOT parse an SSE (`text/event-stream`) response. The endpoint is the binding {mcp_url}; the host provisions ducknng + TLS and gates live external egress. Server-pushed notifications or bidirectional subscriptions are a separate ducknng `wss` concern.

resolvers.title: SQL materialize
resolvers.description: Materialize a table from a read-only SELECT (an MCP JSON-RPC POST via ducknng_ncurl_table).
